ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "middle_name" TEXT /* Middle name of the employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the employee's account */,
  "account_type" INTEGER /* Type of the employee's account (1 for full-time, 2 for part-time, 3 for contract, etc.) */,
  "password_digest" TEXT /* Encrypted password for the employee's account */,
  "home_phone" TEXT /* Home phone number of the employee */,
  "cell_phone" TEXT /* Cell phone number of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Home address of the employee */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager the employee reports to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "benefits_eligibility" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the employee is eligible for benefits */,
  "performance_rating" INTEGER /* Performance rating of the employee */,
  "last_performance_review_date" TEXT /* Date of the last performance review */,
  "last_salary_increase_date" TEXT /* Date of the last salary increase */,
  "last_promotion_date" TEXT /* Date of the last promotion */,
  "pay_rate" REAL /* Hourly pay rate of the employee */,
  "started_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the employee started working */,
  "ended_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the employee ended working (null if still employed) */,
  "archived" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the employee's record is archived */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the employee record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the employee record was last updated */,
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user role assigned to the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the department */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the department record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the department record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user role record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user role record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compensation_history" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compensation entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "new_pay_rate" REAL /* New pay rate after the change */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the new pay rate becomes effective */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the pay rate change */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the compensation record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the compensation record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
